name: quetrex-architect
description: Use when implementing new features in Quetrex. Ensures TDD, TypeScript strict mode, Next.js App Router patterns, ShadCN UI components, and security best practices are followed. Updated for November 2025 standards.
capabilities: Validates TypeScript strict mode compliance for all code
Enforces Next.js Server and Client Component patterns
Checks Zod validation on all API route inputs
Verifies ShadCN UI component usage for consistent design
Requires TDD workflow with tests written before implementation
Identifies hardcoded secrets and SQL injection risks
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill providing architectural guidance. Contains no executable code, no network calls, no file writes, and no external command execution. Static findings are false positives from the analyzer misinterpreting documentation patterns and metadata fields. Previous audit correctly rated this skill as safe.
valueStatement: Inconsistent coding standards create technical debt, security vulnerabilities, and maintenance challenges. This skill enforces TDD workflow, TypeScript strict mode, Next.js App Router patterns, and ShadCN UI components for every new feature in Quetrex.
limitations: Only provides guidance, does not automatically fix code
Requires CLAUDE.md to exist for full project context
Does not execute tests, only describes TDD requirements

useCases.title: Next.js Component Patterns
useCases.description: Ensure new React components follow Server Component defaults and Client Component directives.

useCases.title: API Route Validation
useCases.description: Validate all API endpoints use Zod schemas for input validation and parameterized queries.

useCases.title: Test Coverage Enforcement
useCases.description: Verify code meets coverage thresholds before merge: 75 percent overall, 90 percent for services.

promptTemplates.title: New Feature Setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new feature with TDD
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up a new feature using TDD. Read CLAUDE.md first, then guide me through writing tests before implementation.

promptTemplates.title: Component Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my new component. Check if it correctly uses Server versus Client Components, follows ShadCN patterns, and has proper TypeScript types.

promptTemplates.title: API Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Check my API route. Ensure it has Zod validation for all input, uses parameterized queries, and properly handles errors.

promptTemplates.title: Security Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan my code for hardcoded secrets, SQL injection, XSS vulnerabilities, and missing authentication checks.

bestPractices: Write failing tests before writing implementation code
Use type guards instead of any for unknown data
Validate all API input with Zod schemas
antiPatterns: Using any type to bypass TypeScript errors
Skipping Zod validation for quick API endpoints
Mixing Server and Client Component responsibilities

faq.question: What tools does this skill work with?
faq.answer: Compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Uses Read, Grep, and Glob tools to analyze code.

faq.question: Does this skill write code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and pattern examples. You must implement fixes yourself following the recommendations.

faq.question: What coverage thresholds are enforced?
faq.answer: 75 percent overall, 90 percent for services and utilities, 60 percent for UI components. Adjust based on project needs.

faq.question: How does this integrate with existing workflows?
faq.answer: Call the skill when creating features, reviewing PRs, or refactoring. It reads project context and provides actionable feedback.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads files using standard Claude tools. No code is sent externally or stored anywhere.

faq.question: How is this different from a linter?
faq.answer: Linters catch syntax issues. This skill enforces architectural patterns, workflow practices, and design decisions that linters cannot detect.
